commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow code repair left for ClawSweeper automation in the current patch.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff only changes memory-core filtering/tests and does not touch dependencies, scripts, secrets, permissions, or publishing surfaces. (sha=d48c89618a991117e9c9b0c90aded36405685238)
commands.review_summary:
The branch imports the SDK archive classifier, skips archived session artifacts during memory-core Dreaming corpus collection, and extends the corpus regression test for ordinary reset archives.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +24. Total +28 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source reproduction. Current main feeds Dreaming from the usage-counted session-file listing, which includes reset/deleted archives, and the PR adds the missing Dreaming-side archive boundary.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof before merge; there is no narrow code repair left for ClawSweeper automation in the current patch.

target.title: fix(memory-core): exclude archived transcripts from Dreaming session corpus
target.body:
## Summary

Archived session transcripts (`.jsonl.reset.*` and `.jsonl.deleted.*`) are
currently ingested into the Dreaming corpus, polluting analysis with
reset/deleted cron-spawned subagent noise. On a cron-heavy deployment,
this causes 67-91% of the daily corpus to be dominated by automation
noise, suppressing pattern discovery. This is particularly visible on
workspaces where cron jobs frequently spawn subagents whose transcripts get
reset/deleted but remain in the session directory as usage-counted archives.

## Changes

- Reuse the SDK-exported `isSessionArchiveArtifactName` classifier so Dreaming
  follows the canonical archive filename contract instead of carrying a local regex
- Add archive-exclusion filter in `collectSessionIngestionBatches` so
  Dreaming corpus assembly skips archived transcripts
- Preserve `memory_search` archive indexing path unchanged (only Dreaming
  ingestion is affected)
- Add corpus-level regression coverage for fresh ordinary reset archives,
  including archive timestamps without milliseconds (`...T18-10-00Z`)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Before the fix, `listSessionFilesForAgent` returns
usage-counted `.jsonl.reset.*` / `.jsonl.deleted.*` archive transcript files and
Dreaming ingests them into `memory/.dreams/session-corpus`. After the fix,
Dreaming skips those archive artifacts at the corpus assembly boundary while
leaving the memory_search / usage-counted archive indexing path unchanged.

Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, branch
`fix/dreaming-exclude-archives`, commit `d48c89618a`, local Linux worktree under
`/datad/github/openclaw-issue90313`, using the repository Vitest shard runner and
memory-core plugin harness.

Exact after-fix validation command:

```shell
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts
git diff --check
```

After-fix Dreaming corpus boundary exercised by the regression:

```ts
await fs.writeFile(
  path.join(sessionsDir, "ordinary-archive.jsonl.reset.2026-04-16T18-10-00Z"),
  /* contains: "Archive-only migration detail should not enter Dreaming corpus." */,
);

await beforeAgentReply(
  { cleanedBody: "__openclaw_memory_core_light_sleep__" },
  { trigger: "heartbeat", workspaceDir },
);

const corpus = await fs.readFile(
  path.join(workspaceDir, "memory", ".dreams", "session-corpus", "2026-04-16.txt"),
  "utf-8",
);
expect(corpus).toContain("User: Document the Ollama provider setup.");
expect(corpus).not.toContain("Archive-only migration detail");
expect(corpus).not.toContain("reset archive response");
```

Terminal evidence after the fix:

```text
--- oxlint ---
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 77ms on 2 files with 208 rules using 8 threads.
--- targeted test-projects dreaming phases ---
✓ extension-memory extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts (45 tests) 4466ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       45 passed (45)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 53.76s
--- host sdk session files ---
✓ unit-fast packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts (12 tests) 126ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       12 passed (12)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 45.36s
```

Observed result after fix: The generated Dreaming session corpus keeps the live
ordinary transcript lines (`Document the Ollama provider setup`) and excludes the
fresh reset archive lines (`Archive-only migration detail`, `reset archive
response`). The archive classifier is now the SDK-exported canonical
`isSessionArchiveArtifactName`, so reset/deleted timestamp policy stays aligned
with host session handling. The host SDK session-file tests still pass, preserving
archive indexing for memory_search / usage-counted session history.

What was not tested: A live provider-backed Dream narrative generation run is out
of scope; the changed runtime boundary is the light-Dreaming corpus assembly path,
which the memory-core harness executes directly.

## Test Plan

1. `git diff --check`
2. `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
3. `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts` — 45 tests, all passing
4. `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts` — 12 tests, all passing

## Risk

- Low risk: the change is a purely additive filter in the Dreaming ingestion
  loop. Archive files are still indexed for `memory_search` through the
  unchanged `listSessionFilesForAgent` → `buildSessionEntry` path used by
  `manager-sync-ops.ts` and `qmd-manager.ts`.
- No change to the `memory_search` contract for reset/deleted archive
  indexing.

## Related Issue

Refs #90313 (archive transcript Dreaming corpus pollution).
Note: the broader cron-descended subagent classification issue mentioned in
the linked report is outside this patch's scope and remains tracked separately.
target.author: xiaobao-k8s

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
